name: Gary's Chocolate Cream Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Make a double batch of Gary's Chocolate Pudding, increasing the cornstarch to about 4 1/2 tablespoons per batch (8-10 total). Chill thoroughly. This pudding will be extremely thick.
2. Make or purchase your favorite pie shell. I personally like the good old-fashioned, plain pie shell. My family seems to prefer a cookie-type of shell. If you make your own, pre-bake it as this pie requires no baking.
3. Whip cream with confectioner's sugar and vanilla extract until thick and fluffy.
4. Add 1/2 of the whipped cream to the chocolate pudding. Use an electric mixer for this task to ensure thorough incorporation.
5. Fill the pie shell with the pudding-whipped cream mixture.
6. Place the remaining whipped cream into a piping bag and pipe onto the top of the pie.
7. Shave bittersweet chocolate on top as garnish.
8. Cover the pie and place it in the refrigerator for 2-3 hours to allow the cornstarch from the confectioner's sugar to set.
